title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal direct risk; however, widespread adoption without proper vetting could lead to exposure if the tool has its own vulnerabilities or is misused. Additionally, reliance on the tool might create a false sense of security if it misses critical vulnerabilities.
recommended_actions: Conduct a thorough security review of the tool's source code before integrating it into workflows.
Use the tool as a supplementary measure alongside other established vulnerability scanning solutions.
Monitor for updates or patches to the tool itself to address any potential vulnerabilities discovered post-release.

title: A security flaw has been discovered in Shibby Tomato up to 1.28.0000. Affected by this issue is...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in Shibby Tomato up to 1.28.0000. Affected by this issue is the function sub_2D568 of the component start_jffs2. Performing a manipulation of the argument jffs2_exec results in os command injection. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. This project is superseded by FreshTomato.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-15546.

title: A flaw was found in the vllm-orchestrator-gateway component. The system's production binary logs...
description: A flaw was found in the vllm-orchestrator-gateway component. The system's production binary logs all incoming authorization headers and full chat payloads, which may contain personally identifiable information (PII) and secrets, to persistent logs. This sensitive data, including bearer tokens and chat content, can be accessed by any user with logging privileges. This vulnerability leads to information disclosure, potentially allowing an attacker to harvest credentials and sensitive conversation

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-15574.

title: Mattermost versions 11.7.x <= 11.7.2, 11.6.x <= 11.6.4 fail to verify whether a guest account is...
description: Mattermost versions 11.7.x <= 11.7.2, 11.6.x <= 11.6.4 fail to verify whether a guest account is deactivated before creating a session in the magic-link token login path, which allows a deactivated guest user to obtain a fully functional session via a magic-link token issued prior to deactivation.. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2026-00681

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows deactivated guest accounts to regain access using a previously issued magic-link token, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive information or systems.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to the latest patched version of Mattermost that addresses this vulnerability (above 11.7.2 or 11.6.4).
Revoke all active magic-link tokens issued prior to the patch to prevent unauthorized access.
Review and monitor guest account activity for any suspicious behavior or unauthorized access attempts.

title: Mattermost versions 11.7.x <= 11.7.2, 11.6.x <= 11.6.4, 10.11.x <= 10.11.19 fail to validate that...
description: Mattermost versions 11.7.x <= 11.7.2, 11.6.x <= 11.6.4, 10.11.x <= 10.11.19 fail to validate that an assigned incoming webhook user has access to the target team or channel, which allows a requester with webhook management permissions to create posts or direct messages attributed to another user via crafted incoming webhook configuration and payloads.. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2026-00683

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows unauthorized users with webhook management permissions to impersonate other users and post messages or direct messages in Mattermost. This could lead to misinformation, social engineering attacks, or disruption of communication channels.
recommended_actions: Upgrade Mattermost to the latest patched version that addresses CVE-2026-9708.
Restrict webhook management permissions to trusted users and review existing permissions.
Monitor webhook activity logs for any suspicious or unauthorized behavior.

title: Mattermost versions 11.7.x <= 11.7.2, 11.6.x <= 11.6.4, 10.11.x <= 10.11.19 fail to invalidate...
description: Mattermost versions 11.7.x <= 11.7.2, 11.6.x <= 11.6.4, 10.11.x <= 10.11.19 fail to invalidate OAuth refresh tokens upon user account deactivation, which allows a deactivated user or an attacker in possession of a valid refresh token to obtain new functional access tokens via the OAuth refresh token grant endpoint.. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2026-00680

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9571.

title: A weakness has been identified in Shibby Tomato up to 1.28.0000. This affects the function...
description: A weakness has been identified in Shibby Tomato up to 1.28.0000. This affects the function sub_2D048 of the component CIFS Mount Handler. Executing a manipulation of the argument cifs1/cifs2 can lead to os command injection. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. This project is superseded by FreshTomato.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The identified vulnerability in Shibby Tomato up to version 1.28.0000 allows for OS command injection via the CIFS Mount Handler. This could enable attackers to execute arbitrary commands on the affected system remotely, potentially compromising the device and its network. However, the severity is mitigated by the fact that the project is deprecated and superseded by FreshTomato.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to FreshTomato or a supported firmware version to mitigate the vulnerability.
Disable or restrict CIFS Mount functionality if upgrading is not immediately possible.
Implement network segmentation and firewall rules to limit exposure of affected devices to the internet.

title: Mattermost versions 11.7.x <= 11.7.2, 11.6.x <= 11.6.4, 10.11.x <= 10.11.19 fail to validate the...
description: Mattermost versions 11.7.x <= 11.7.2, 11.6.x <= 11.6.4, 10.11.x <= 10.11.19 fail to validate the length and content of message attachment field values, which allows an authenticated attacker to cause a denial of service for all users in a channel via a post containing a specially crafted payload that triggers catastrophic backtracking in the client-side markdown parser.. Mattermost Advisory ID: MMSA-2026-00658

title: A Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) protection bypass existed in the html_to_markdown expansion...
description:
A Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) protection bypass existed in the html_to_markdown expansion module of misp-modules.

The module attempts to prevent requests to loopback, private, link-local, and other restricted IP address ranges. However, IP addresses were compared against the blocked ranges without first normalising IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses.

An authenticated attacker able to invoke the module could supply an IPv4-mapped IPv6 address, such as:

http://[::ffff:127.0.0.1]/
http://[::ffff:

title: A vulnerability was found in Tenda CH22 1.0.0.1. This impacts the function formCertListInfo of...
description: A vulnerability was found in Tenda CH22 1.0.0.1. This impacts the function formCertListInfo of the file /goform/CertListInfo. The manipulation of the argument Name results in buffer overflow. The attack can be launched remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Tenda CH22 1.0.0.1 allows for a buffer overflow via the formCertListInfo function, potentially enabling remote attackers to execute arbitrary code, compromise devices, or cause service disruptions.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply any available patches or firmware updates from Tenda to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement network segmentation to isolate vulnerable devices and reduce the attack surface.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and consider deploying intrusion detection/prevention systems (IDS/IPS).

title: A vulnerability was determined in Shibby Tomato up to 1.28.0000. Affected is the function...
description: A vulnerability was determined in Shibby Tomato up to 1.28.0000. Affected is the function getupsvar of the file www/apcupsd/tomatodata.cgi of the component apcupsd. This manipulation of the argument Field causes stack-based buffer overflow. The attack may be initiated remotely. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. This project is superseded by FreshTomato.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Shibby Tomato up to version 1.28.0000 allows remote attackers to exploit a stack-based buffer overflow via the getupsvar function in the apcupsd component. This could lead to remote code execution, system compromise, or denial of service. Since the exploit has been publicly disclosed, the risk of exploitation is significantly increased.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade to FreshTomato or another actively maintained firmware to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement network segmentation to isolate vulnerable devices and reduce exposure to external threats.
Monitor network traffic for signs of exploitation and apply intrusion detection/prevention systems to block suspicious activity.

title: This vulnerability is a critical Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) in Centreon's centreon...
description: This vulnerability is a critical Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) in Centreon's centreon-open-tickets module that leads to Remote Code Execution. The message_confirm field is stored without sanitization and rendered via Smarty with no security policy enabled, allowing any authenticated user, to inject and execute arbitrary code on the server. This results in disclosure of environment secrets and could impact platform availability of Centreon Infra Monitoring product.

title: Certain devices in the WAGO System I/O Field series activate an internal diagnostic capability...
description: Certain devices in the WAGO System I/O Field series activate an internal diagnostic capability during the initial startup sequence. This functionality is not formally documented and becomes accessible without authentication for a brief period in the early boot phase. During this window, an unauthenticated remote attacker can gain access to the internal system processes, resulting in full system compromise.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated remote attackers to exploit undocumented diagnostic functionality during the boot phase, potentially leading to full system compromise. This could result in unauthorized control, data theft, or disruption of critical operations.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply firmware updates or patches provided by WAGO to address CVE-2026-4769, if available.
Implement network segmentation to isolate vulnerable devices from external access and restrict access to trusted systems only.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity during device boot sequences and deploy intrusion detection systems to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title: The Joomla extension Helix Ultimate is vulnerable to an unauthenticated arbitrary file deletion.
description: The Joomla extension Helix Ultimate is vulnerable to an unauthenticated arbitrary file deletion.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Joomla extension Helix Ultimate allows unauthenticated attackers to delete arbitrary files on the server, potentially leading to service disruption, data loss, or unauthorized access to sensitive information.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Helix Ultimate extension to the latest patched version if available.
Implement web application firewall (WAF) rules to detect and block exploitation attempts targeting this vulnerability.
Regularly back up critical files and ensure backups are stored securely to mitigate potential data loss.

title: CVE-2026-14794 - A flaw has been found in Craft CMS up to 4.18.0.1. Affected by this vulnerability is the function ac
description: A flaw has been found in Craft CMS up to 4.18.0.1. Affected by this vulnerability is the function actionGetNewUsersData of the file src/controllers/ChartsController.php of the component Charts Endpoint. This manipulation of the argument userGroupId causes improper authorization. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. Upgrading to version 4.18.1 addresses this issue. Patch name: 9ee53efc1314e6aba32771c66a13e072a246f4ce. It is suggested to upgrade the affected component.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Craft CMS allows improper authorization via the Charts Endpoint, potentially exposing sensitive user data or enabling unauthorized access to user group information. This could lead to data breaches or privilege escalation attacks.
affected_industries: Small and Medium Businesses using Craft CMS
recommended_actions: Upgrade Craft CMS to version 4.18.1 or later to apply the patch addressing this vulnerability.
Review access logs for any suspicious activity related to the Charts Endpoint to identify potential exploitation attempts.
Implement additional authorization checks for sensitive endpoints as a temporary mitigation measure until the patch is applied.

title: CVE-2026-14793 - A vulnerability was detected in Craft CMS up to 4.18.0.1. Affected is the function actionReorderSets
description: A vulnerability was detected in Craft CMS up to 4.18.0.1. Affected is the function actionReorderSets of the file src/controllers/GlobalsController.php of the component reorder-sets Endpoint. The manipulation results in authorization bypass. The attack can be executed remotely. Upgrading to version 4.18.1 is able to address this issue. The patch is identified as 9bd05c91e6a7e6da5e949ec41a31c220c059aa04. The affected component should be upgraded.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Craft CMS allows for authorization bypass via the actionReorderSets function, potentially enabling unauthorized users to manipulate or access restricted global settings. This could lead to data exposure, unauthorized changes, or system misconfigurations.
recommended_actions: Upgrade Craft CMS to version 4.18.1 or later to apply the patch identified as 9bd05c91e6a7e6da5e949ec41a31c220c059aa04.
Review access logs for any unauthorized activity targeting the reorder-sets endpoint to identify potential exploitation.
Implement additional access controls and monitoring for sensitive endpoints to mitigate the risk of future authorization bypass vulnerabilities.

title: CVE-2026-14803 - Mojo::JSON versions before 9.47 for Perl allow memory exhaustion via unbounded recursion in the pure
description:
Mojo::JSON versions before 9.47 for Perl allow memory exhaustion via unbounded recursion in the pure-Perl decoder.

The pure-Perl decode path (`_decode_value` dispatching to `_decode_array` and `_decode_object`) recurses with no depth limit, so a small deeply nested JSON document can consume excessive memory.

This path is the default when Cpanel::JSON::XS is not installed or `MOJO_NO_JSON_XS=1` is set; the Cpanel::JSON::XS fast path is not affected.

Any caller that decodes an untrusted JSON body, for example `Mojo::Message::json` reached through `$c->req->json`, can exhaust process memory and cause denial of service.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-14803.
